race.name: Granfondo La Guadeloupe
race.tagline: Tropical UCI Gran Fondo qualifier with Caribbean climbs and sea views.

climate.description: Tropical Caribbean climate in December, warm and lush during northern winter with reduced heat and humidity.

features: Undulating coastal roads with major mountain climbs on Basse-Terre side

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 74. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 69.

course_description.character: Coastal undulations to major climb, then rolling west with smaller ascent; tropical mountain roads with sea and volcano views.
suffering_zones: Col des Mamelles: 13.5km at 4.3% avg (4km >8.5%, max 620m)

logistics.transport: Accessible via major international airport; registrations via sportsnconnect.com until Nov 30, 2025; 86 hotels plus alternatives.

history.origin_story: New 2025 event, first edition Dec 6-7; UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier organized by Team Cama CCD and OD'ASS as Caribbean license holders.

biased_opinion.summary: Brand new UCI Gran Fondo in a stunning tropical setting—perfect for winter training with Worlds qualifier spots and post-race beach recovery. The Col des Mamelles climb delivers the pain amid paradise vistas; pair it with the discovery ride for full island immersion. Logistics favor Europeans, but worth the trip for adventure seekers.

final_verdict.one_liner: Exotic UCI qualifier blending steep climbs and Caribbean allure.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Granfondo La Guadeloupe stands out as a fresh 2025 addition to the calendar, offering 124km/2883m of tropical road suffering with top-25% age-group qualification to 2026 Worlds in Japan. Signature 13.5km Col des Mamelles (4km at >8.5%) anchors a scenic Basse-Terre loop from Capesterre-Belle-Eau. Ideal for amateurs seeking prestige points in paradise, with expo, non-competitive 80km option, and abundant tourism. Travel via Pointe-à-Pitre airport; French infrastructure ensures quality support.
